subject: Math
skill: Tell and Write Time to Hour and Half-Hour

lesson.story_mission: Set the Clock Tower for school, lunch, play, and bedtime using hour and half-hour times.
lesson.objective: I can tell and write time to hour and half-hour.
lesson.concept_explanation: Tell and Write Time to Hour and Half-Hour means using a math model, saying what you see, and checking that the answer fits.
lesson.common_misconception: The short hand points near the hour.
lesson.real_world_connection: Set the Clock Tower for school, lunch, play, and bedtime using hour and half-hour times.
lesson.lesson_summary: Use the model and careful counting to solve tell and write time to hour and half-hour problems.
lesson.mini_lesson: Use analog clock to reason carefully before choosing or writing an answer.

worked_examples.text: Watch the model first, then match it to the answer for tell and write time to hour and half-hour.
Demo: count, compare, or match each part before answering.

hint_ladder: Say the math words out loud.
Check your answer by doing the action again.

guided_practice.prompt: What time is shown on the clock?
hints: Look at the model carefully.
Count or compare one step at a time.
Check that your answer matches the question.
guided_practice.explanation: The short hour hand shows 1; the long minute hand shows 00 minutes, so the time is 1:00.

misconception_bank.hour_hand_confusion: The short hand points near the hour.
misconception_bank.half_hour_confusion: Half past means 30 minutes after the hour.
misconception_bank.hand_length_confusion: The hour hand is shorter and the minute hand is longer.
misconception_bank.reads_minute_as_hour: Read the hour hand first, then the minute hand.

story.text: Welcome to Clock Tower Quest. The Clock Tower needs help setting times for school, lunch, play, and bedtime. Your goal is to tell and write time to the hour and half-hour. You will learn how the short hour hand and the long minute hand work together. Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This lesson is about telling and writing time to the hour and half-hour. Important words are clock, hour hand, minute hand, o clock, half past, and thirty. The analog clock model shows a short hand for the hour and a long hand for the minutes. A common mistake is reading only the long hand, so check where the short hand points too. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: Watch this worked example. The prompt asks what time is shown on the clock, and the analog clock model shows an hour hand and a minute hand. Step one, look at the short hour hand to find the hour. Step two, look at the long minute hand: at the 12 it means zero minutes, and at the 6 it means thirty minutes. Step three, say the time with math words. The final answer can be 1 o clock when the hour hand shows 1 and the minute hand shows zero minutes. Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This demo shows how an analog clock tells time. Watch the short hand for the hour and the long hand for the minutes. For example, when the short hand points to 1 and the long hand points to 12, the time is 1 o clock. Press Next to continue.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: This is practice. Use the clock strategy: read the short hour hand first, then read the long minute hand. The model shows an analog clock, such as 1 o clock with zero minutes. You can press Read Question to hear the exact question again. Choose your answer when you are ready.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: This is a challenge. It may show a half-hour time, where the minute hand points to 6 and the hour hand is between two numbers. Use the short hand to find the hour and the long hand to decide zero minutes or thirty minutes. You can press Read Question to hear the exact question, and use the hint if you need help.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: This is your checkpoint. Show that you can tell time to the hour and half-hour. Read the hour hand, then the minute hand, and choose the time that matches the clock model. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Answer carefully, then continue.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: Great work in Clock Tower Quest. You practiced reading hour hands, minute hands, o clock times, and half-hour times. Telling time helps you follow daily routines. Press Next to see your profile.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This is your growth profile. It can summarize your attempts, correct answers, and accuracy so you know how your time practice went. Your next recommendation will help you keep reading clocks. You can replay the mission or go back to the hub.
profile.label: Read This Page
